---
title: "Ch1_SW_Differences"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r,echo=FALSE}
  knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dataRetrieval)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(zoo) #for moving window
library(stringr)
library(vioplot)
library(purrr)
library(lavaan)
library(blavaan)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(lme4)
library(broom.mixed)
```


The purpose of this script is to:

    - ORGANIZE DATA 
    - Import and arrange .csv files of finalized paired sample data

    - TEST MODELs
    - Model difference in SWA between dammed and undammed pairs as a function of
      - Dam Presence
      - Topographic Openness
      - Riparian Area
      - Contributing Drainage Area
      
    - Model 1: Dam Presence Only
    - Model 2: Dam Presence + Context
      
----

Read in Flow data from USGS LBV Guston Coulee gage:

```{r,echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# LOWER BEAVER CK
# Gage near Guston Coulee
usgsGage_06166000 <- readNWISdv(
  siteNumbers = "06166000",
  parameterCd = "00060",
  startDate = "2019-06-01",
  endDate   = "2023-10-01"
)

###########################################

# Clean USGS
usgs_df <- usgsGage_06166000 %>%
  mutate(
    source = "USGS",
    date   = as.Date(Date),
    flow   = X_00060_00003
    ) %>%
  filter(
    (date >= as.Date("2019-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2019-10-01")) |
    (date >= as.Date("2021-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2021-10-01")) |
    (date >= as.Date("2023-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2023-10-01")) ) %>% 
  mutate(
    zFlow = scale(flow)
  ) %>% 
  select(source,
         date,
         zFlow) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = case_when(between(date, as.Date("2019-06-01"), as.Date("2019-06-14")) ~ 191,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-06-15"), as.Date("2019-06-30")) ~ 192,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-07-01"), as.Date("2019-07-14")) ~ 193,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-07-15"), as.Date("2019-07-31")) ~ 194,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-08-01"), as.Date("2019-08-14")) ~ 195,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-08-15"), as.Date("2019-08-31")) ~ 196,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-09-01"), as.Date("2019-09-14")) ~ 197,
                                between(date, as.Date("2019-09-15"), as.Date("2019-09-30")) ~ 198,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-06-01"), as.Date("2021-06-14")) ~ 211,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-06-15"), as.Date("2021-06-30")) ~ 212,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2021-07-14")) ~ 213,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-07-15"), as.Date("2021-07-31")) ~ 214,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-08-01"), as.Date("2021-08-14")) ~ 215,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-08-15"), as.Date("2021-08-31")) ~ 216,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-09-01"), as.Date("2021-09-14")) ~ 217,
                                between(date, as.Date("2021-09-15"), as.Date("2021-09-30")) ~ 218,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-06-01"), as.Date("2023-06-14")) ~ 231,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-06-15"), as.Date("2023-06-30")) ~ 232,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-07-01"), as.Date("2023-07-14")) ~ 233,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-07-15"), as.Date("2023-07-31")) ~ 234,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-08-01"), as.Date("2023-08-14")) ~ 235,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-08-15"), as.Date("2023-08-31")) ~ 236,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-09-01"), as.Date("2023-09-14")) ~ 237,
                                between(date, as.Date("2023-09-15"), as.Date("2023-09-30")) ~ 238,
                                TRUE ~ NA_real_)) %>% 
  filter(surveyPeriod > 0)

usgs_df$surveyPeriod <- as.factor(usgs_df$surveyPeriod)


mean_flow_per_survey_period <- usgs_df %>% 
  group_by(surveyPeriod) %>%
  summarise(zFlow_mean = mean(zFlow),
            .groups = "drop")



lateSummerFlow <- usgs_df %>% 
  filter(surveyPeriod != 191,
         surveyPeriod != 192,
         surveyPeriod != 193,
         surveyPeriod != 194,
         surveyPeriod != 211,
         surveyPeriod != 212,
         surveyPeriod != 213,
         surveyPeriod != 214,
         surveyPeriod != 231,
         surveyPeriod != 232,
         surveyPeriod != 233,
         surveyPeriod != 234,
         )
```


2019:
----

High early/mid-summer flow followed by moderate/low late season. 

```{r,echo=FALSE}

flow19 <- lateSummerFlow %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  filter(
    (date >= as.Date("2019-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2019-10-01"))
  )


####

ggplot(flow19, aes(x = date, 
                       y = zFlow,
                       group = lubridate::year(date))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)",
    title = "Summer '19 Stream Discharge; USGS 06166000; Lower Beaver",
    subtitle = "z-scaled June through September, 2019, 2021, 2023"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

```

----
  
2021:
----

Low flow conditions throughout the summer season. Part of 3 year sustained drought conditions (2020, 2021, 2022).



```{r,echo=FALSE}

flow21 <- lateSummerFlow %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  filter(
    (date >= as.Date("2021-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2021-10-01"))
  )


####

ggplot(flow21, aes(x = date, 
                       y = zFlow,
                       group = lubridate::year(date))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)",
    title = "Summer '21 Stream Discharge; USGS 06166000; Lower Beaver",
    subtitle = "z-scaled June through September, 2019, 2021, 2023"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

```  
  
----

2023:
----

High early flow conditions followed by moderate to lower late summer. 



```{r,echo=FALSE}
flow23 <- lateSummerFlow %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  filter(
    (date >= as.Date("2023-06-01") & date <= as.Date("2023-10-01"))
  )


####

ggplot(flow23, aes(x = date, 
                       y = zFlow,
                       group = lubridate::year(date))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)",
    title = "Summer '23 Stream Discharge; USGS 06166000; Lower Beaver",
    subtitle = "z-scaled June through September, 2019, 2021, 2023"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

```  





```{r, echo=FALSE}

#Read in data frames for each HUC12/Year combo:

# WILLOW:
#2019:
wc_19 <- read.csv("WC_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'WC')
#2021:
wc_21 <- read.csv("WC_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'WC')
#2023:
wc_23 <- read.csv("WC_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'WC')
#AllYears:
WC <- bind_rows(wc_19,wc_21,wc_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))
  
# LONE TREE:
#2019:
lt_19 <- read.csv("LT_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'LT')
#2021:
lt_21 <- read.csv("LT_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'LT')
#2023:
lt_23 <- read.csv("LT_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv")%>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'LT')

#AllYears:
LT <- bind_rows(lt_19,lt_21,lt_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))

# BRAZIL:
#2019:
brz_19 <- read.csv("BRZ_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'BRZ')
#2021:
brz_21 <- read.csv("BRZ_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'BRZ')
#2023:
brz_23 <- read.csv("BRZ_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'BRZ')

#AllYears:
BRZ <- bind_rows(brz_19,brz_21,brz_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))
  
# LARB:
#2019:
lrb_19 <- read.csv("LRB_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'LRB')
#2021:
lrb_21 <- read.csv("LRB_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv")%>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'LRB')
#2023:
lrb_23 <- read.csv("LRB_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'LRB')

#AllYears:
LRB <- bind_rows(lrb_19,lrb_21,lrb_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))
  
# LOWER BEAVER:
#2019:
lbv_19 <- read.csv("LBV_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'LBV')
#2021:
lbv_21 <- read.csv("LBV_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'LBV')
#2023:
lbv_23 <- read.csv("LBV_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'LBV')

#AllYears:
LBV <- bind_rows(lbv_19,lbv_21,lbv_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))
  
# UPPER BEAVER:
#2019:
ubv_19 <- read.csv("UBV_2019_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2019,
         watershed = 'UBV')
#2021:
ubv_21 <- read.csv("UBV_2021_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2021,
         watershed = 'UBV')
#2023:
ubv_23 <- read.csv("UBV_2023_DF_Final_Long_0427Update_250Spacing.csv") %>% 
  mutate(year = 2023,
         watershed = 'UBV')

#AllYears:
UBV <- bind_rows(ubv_19,ubv_21,ubv_23) %>% 
  mutate(surveyPeriod = as.factor(case_when(year == 2019 & t == 1 ~ 191,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 2 ~ 192,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 3 ~ 193,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 4 ~ 194,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 5 ~ 195,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 6 ~ 196,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 7 ~ 197,
                                  year == 2019 & t == 8 ~ 198,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 1 ~ 211,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 2 ~ 212,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 3 ~ 213,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 4 ~ 214,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 5 ~ 215,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 6 ~ 216,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 7 ~ 217,
                                  year == 2021 & t == 8 ~ 218,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 1 ~ 231,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 2 ~ 232,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 3 ~ 233,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 4 ~ 234,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 5 ~ 235,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 6 ~ 236,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 7 ~ 237,
                                  year == 2023 & t == 8 ~ 238)))
```


----

Currently each incoming data frame is of the following structure:

```{r,echo=FALSE}
str(brz_19)
```

Where:

    - clusterID_seq = dammed/undammed sample pair ID
    - window_type = whether a sample window is 'dammed' or 'undammed'
    - watershed = watershed identifier (e.g., "BRZ" = Brazil Ck)
    - year = year
    - window_length = length (m) of stream flow line within sample window
    - catchment_km2 = contributing catchment area/upstream drainage area (km^2)
    - riparian_area_m2 = riparian area (m^2) of sample window; 2-m vertical
      buffer from DEM low point (stream flow line)
    - TO = mean topographic openness across window
    - p_window: avg dam detection prob for target window; see CP Ch1
    - f_window: avg dam false positive prob for target window; see CP Ch1
    - beav_count_all: all unique detected dams
    - beav_count_conf: unique confidently detected dams
    - anthro_count: all unique detected anthropogenic dams
    - isAnthro: National Wetland Inventory (NWI) "dyked/impounded"
    - Nhat = estimated dam count within sample window
      - ...= beav_count_all/(p_window(1-f_window)  [McCallum 2005]
    - TO_mean = mean topographic openness across window
    - time_period = 2wk interval 06/01 - 09/30
    - SWA = surface water area (m^2) for window during time (t)
    - t = time (t) {same as time_period}

----
----
----
----

WILLOW
----
----

Compute **Riparian Inundation**

For survey window[i] at time[t], the proportion of riparian area that is covered in surface water

$SWA = Surface Water Area (m^2)$

$RIPA = Riparian Area (m^2)$

$$RI[i,t] = SWA[i,t] / RIPA[i] $$


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# COMPUTE RI HERE!!!
WC_RI <- WC %>% 
  mutate(RI = SWA/riparian_area_m2)
```

Visualize riparian inundation **(RI)** $SWA/RIPA$ between dammed and undammed across years.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
WC_RI %>%
  filter(!is.na(RI)) %>%   # optional but recommended
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year),
             y = RI,
             fill = window_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                               "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    fill = "Window type",
    title = "RI across dammed vs undammed windows",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek (2019, 2021, 2023)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
WC_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Willow Ck; Summer 2019") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
WC_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2021) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Willow Ck; Summer 2021") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
WC_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Willow Ck; Summer 2023") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

----

Add **relative flow data**. 

NOTE: currently this represents data from the USGS Guston Coulee gage that has been z-standardized over the current data acquisition range (June-Sept; 2019, 2021, 2023). 

Then, for each 2-week survey time period (1:8), the average relative flow $[mean(zFlow)]$ was computed, and added as a data column. IT IS ASSUMED that this flow data is representative of the RELATIVE flow pattern experienced throughout the study area during this time. Consider incorporating relative HHP simulated flow data again (?)

----

Visualize Surface Water Area across Relative Flow and Topographic Openness


```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
WC_RelFlow <- WC_RI %>% 
  left_join(mean_flow_per_survey_period,
            by = "surveyPeriod") %>% 
  mutate(dam_m = Nhat/window_length)


WC_RelFlow %>% 
  arrange(SWA) %>%
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO, 
           color = SWA,
           shape = window_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "mako",begin = 0,end   = 1,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("Surface Water Area" (m^2)),
    title = "Surface Water Area Across RelativeFlow and TO space",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


----

----  

----

----



Visualize covariate distributions b/t dammed and undammed windows:

```{r,include=FALSE}
ggplot(WC_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = TO, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


ggplot(WC_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = riparian_area_m2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(WC_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = SWA, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(WC_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = RI, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(WC_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = catchment_km2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```



----

Visualize covariate correlations for Willow Creek :

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
#2019
wc19_numCols <- WC_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2019") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
wc19_corr_matrix <- cor(wc19_numCols)

# Create the plot
wc19_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(wc19_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2019") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

wc19_ggcorr_plot

#2021
wc21_numCols <- WC_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2021") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
wc21_corr_matrix <- cor(wc21_numCols)

# Create the plot
wc21_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(wc21_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2021") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

wc21_ggcorr_plot


#2023
wc23_numCols <- WC_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2023") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
wc23_corr_matrix <- cor(wc23_numCols)

# Create the plot
wc23_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(wc23_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2023") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

wc23_ggcorr_plot
```

----

----

----

Compute Differences (Dammed[i] - Undammed):
----

Compute difference in RI $(\Delta RI)$ between dammed and undammed pairs. The hypothesis is that dammed reaches will inundate more of their respective available space (m^2) than their paired undammed reaches. (+)$(\Delta RI)$ means the dammed site in a pair inundated more of its available riparian space than its undammed counterpart; (-) is vice versa.

The degree to which beaver dams influence $\Delta RI$ confounded by pairwise differences in reach characteristics (e.g., $TO$, $UDA$)

----

Proposed model:

$$\Delta RI[i] = \alpha + \beta1 * \Delta UDA[i] + \beta2 * \Delta TO + \beta3 *  Nhat[i_{\text{dammed}}]$$


Wherein, the difference in riparian inundation $(\Delta RI)$ for sample pair $[i]$ is a linear function of the pair's difference in stream network position $(\Delta UDA)$, the pair's difference in local channel confinement $(\Delta TO)$, and the pair's difference in number of dams $(\Delta Nhat)$ (NOTE: delta_Nhat is just Nhat of the dammed site in pair [i]).

Consider, instead of Nhat, using $dam/m = Nhat/windowLength (m)$ to get at dam density within the window, rather than raw dam count.


```{r,echo=FALSE}
wc_sample_pairs <- WC_RelFlow %>%
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         window_type, 
         RI, 
         TO, 
         riparian_area_m2, 
         zFlow_mean,
         catchment_km2,
         Nhat,
         dam_m) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = window_type,
    values_from = c(RI, TO, riparian_area_m2, catchment_km2, Nhat,dam_m),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(delta_RI = RI_dammed - RI_undammed)

wc_sample_differences <- wc_sample_pairs %>%
  mutate(
    delta_TO   = TO_dammed - TO_undammed,
    delta_RIPA = riparian_area_m2_dammed - riparian_area_m2_undammed,
    delta_UDA  = catchment_km2_dammed - catchment_km2_undammed,
    delta_Nhat = Nhat_dammed,
    delta_dam_m = dam_m_dammed
  ) %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         delta_RI,
         delta_TO,
         delta_RIPA,
         delta_UDA,
         delta_Nhat,
         delta_dam_m
         )

str(wc_sample_differences)
```


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across $\Delta Nhat$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

wc_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

wc_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```


----


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across **damDensity** $(dam/m)$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

wc_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

wc_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Willow Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####
```


----

Dig into where $\Delta RI >0$; these illustrate contexts where dams may be positively influencing SWA.

Look at TO and RIPA and UDA for delta_RI > 0 dam clusters

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_posDamDiff <- wc_sample_pairs %>% 
  filter(delta_RI > 0)

wc_posDamDiff$year <- as.factor(wc_posDamDiff$year)

wc_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    shape = "Year",
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and TO"),
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)



wc_sample_differences %>%
  filter(delta_RI >0) %>% 
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = delta_TO, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = as.factor(year))) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Difference in Topographic Openness (  "*Delta*" TO)"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    shape = "Year",
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and " *Delta* "TO"),
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = riparian_area_m2_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Riparian Area ("*m^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and RIPA"),
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = catchment_km2_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Upstream Drainage Area ("*km^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
wc_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = dam_m_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Dam Density (" *DamCount[i]/streamLength(m)[i]* ")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Willow Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```



```{r,echo=TRUE}
wcRI_Flow <- wc_sample_pairs %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         RI_dammed,
         RI_undammed) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(RI_dammed, RI_undammed),
    names_to = "window_type",
    values_to = "RI"
  )

ggplot(wcRI_Flow,
       aes(x = zFlow_mean,
           y = RI,
           color = as.factor(window_type))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RI_dammed" = "brown",
                                "RI_undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    color = "Window Type",
    title = "Riparian Inundation Across Flow",
    subtitle = "WC All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

----
----
----
----




----
----
----
----

LONE TREE   
----
----

Compute **Riparian Inundation**

For survey window[i] at time[t], the proportion of riparian area that is covered in surface water

$SWA = Surface Water Area (m^2)$

$RIPA = Riparian Area (m^2)$

$$RI[i,t] = SWA[i,t] / RIPA[i] $$


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# COMPUTE RI HERE!!!
LT_RI <- LT %>% 
  mutate(RI = SWA/riparian_area_m2)
```

Visualize riparian inundation **(RI)** $SWA/RIPA$ between dammed and undammed across years.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
LT_RI %>%
  filter(!is.na(RI)) %>%   # optional but recommended
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year),
             y = RI,
             fill = window_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                               "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    fill = "Window type",
    title = "RI across dammed vs undammed windows",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek (2019, 2021, 2023)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```


```{r,echo=FALSE}
LT_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Lone Tree; Summer 2019") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
LT_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2021) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Lone Tree; Summer 2021") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
LT_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Lone Tree; Summer 2023") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```


----

Add **relative flow data**. 

----

Visualize Surface Water Area across Relative Flow and Topographic Openness


```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
LT_RelFlow <- LT_RI %>% 
  left_join(mean_flow_per_survey_period,
            by = "surveyPeriod") %>% 
  mutate(dam_m = Nhat/window_length)


LT_RelFlow %>% 
  arrange(SWA) %>%
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO, 
           color = SWA,
           shape = window_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "mako",begin = 0,end   = 1,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("Surface Water Area" (m^2)),
    title = "Surface Water Area Across RelativeFlow and TO space",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


----

----  

----

----



Visualize covariate distributions b/t dammed and undammed windows:

```{r,include=FALSE}
ggplot(LT_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = TO, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


ggplot(LT_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = riparian_area_m2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(LT_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = SWA, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(LT_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = RI, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(LT_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = catchment_km2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```



----

Visualize covariate correlations for Willow Creek :

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
#2019
lt19_numCols <- LT_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2019") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
lt19_corr_matrix <- cor(lt19_numCols)

# Create the plot
lt19_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(lt19_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2019") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

lt19_ggcorr_plot

#2021
lt21_numCols <- LT_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2021") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
lt21_corr_matrix <- cor(lt21_numCols)

# Create the plot
lt21_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(lt21_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2021") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

lt21_ggcorr_plot


#2023
lt23_numCols <- LT_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2023") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
lt23_corr_matrix <- cor(lt23_numCols)

# Create the plot
lt23_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(lt23_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Willow Creek 2023") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

lt23_ggcorr_plot
```

----

----

----

Compute Differences (Dammed[i] - Undammed):
----


```{r,echo=FALSE}
lt_sample_pairs <- LT_RelFlow %>%
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         window_type,
         SWA,
         RI, 
         TO, 
         riparian_area_m2, 
         zFlow_mean,
         catchment_km2,
         Nhat,
         dam_m) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = window_type,
    values_from = c(SWA, RI, TO, riparian_area_m2, catchment_km2, Nhat,dam_m),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(delta_RI = RI_dammed - RI_undammed,
         delta_SWA = SWA_dammed - SWA_undammed)

lt_sample_differences <- lt_sample_pairs %>%
  mutate(
    delta_TO   = TO_dammed - TO_undammed,
    delta_RIPA = riparian_area_m2_dammed - riparian_area_m2_undammed,
    delta_UDA  = catchment_km2_dammed - catchment_km2_undammed,
    delta_Nhat = Nhat_dammed,
    delta_dam_m = dam_m_dammed
  ) %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         delta_SWA,
         delta_RI,
         delta_TO,
         delta_RIPA,
         delta_UDA,
         delta_Nhat,
         delta_dam_m
         )

str(lt_sample_differences)
```


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across $\Delta Nhat$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

lt_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

lt_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```


----


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across **damDensity** $(dam/m)$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

lt_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

lt_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####
```


----

Dig into where $\Delta RI >0$; these illustrate contexts where dams may be positively influencing SWA.

Look at TO and RIPA and UDA for delta_RI > 0 dam clusters

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_posDamDiff <- lt_sample_pairs %>% 
  filter(delta_SWA > 0)

lt_posDamDiff$year <- as.factor(lt_posDamDiff$year)

lt_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_SWA) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO_dammed, 
           color = delta_SWA,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("("*Delta*" SWA) (SWA_dammed - SWA_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaSWA Across Flow and TO"),
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)



lt_sample_differences %>%
  filter(delta_SWA >0) %>% 
  arrange(delta_SWA) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = delta_TO, 
           color = delta_SWA,
           shape = as.factor(year))) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Difference in Topographic Openness (  "*Delta*" TO)"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" SWA) (SWA_dammed - SWA_undammed)"),
    shape = "Year",
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaSWA Across Flow and " *Delta* "TO"),
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_SWA) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = (riparian_area_m2_dammed-riparian_area_m2_undammed), 
           color = delta_SWA,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Difference in Riparian Area ( " *m^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" SWA) (SWA_dammed - SWA_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaSWA Across Flow and RIPA"),
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_SWA) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = (catchment_km2_dammed - catchment_km2_undammed), 
           color = delta_SWA,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Upstream Drainage Area ("*km^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" SWA) (SWA_dammed - SWA_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}

lt_sample_pairs <- lt_sample_pairs %>%
  mutate(
    delta_bin = case_when(
      as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) < 0        ~ "Negative",
      as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) >= 0 & as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) <= 0.5   ~ "None",
      as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) > 0.5 & as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) <  2.5    ~ "Positive_weak",
      as.numeric(scale(delta_SWA)) >= 2.5         ~ "Positive_strong"
    )
  ) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(delta_RI))

lt_sample_pairs$delta_bin <- factor(
  lt_sample_pairs$delta_bin,
  levels = c("Negative", "None", "Positive_weak", "Positive_strong")
)

lt_sample_pairs$year <- as.factor(lt_sample_pairs$year)

lt_sample_pairs <- lt_sample_pairs %>% 
  rename("Year" = "year")

bin_colors <- c(
  Negative = "grey", 
  None   = "grey", 
  Positive_weak   = "lightblue2", 
  Positive_strong = "navy"   
)

lt_sample_pairs %>%
  arrange(delta_SWA) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = as.numeric(scale(riparian_area_m2_dammed - riparian_area_m2_undammed)), 
           y = as.numeric(scale((TO_dammed - TO_undammed))), 
           color = delta_bin,
           shape = Year)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black") +
  geom_point(
  aes(
    x = as.numeric(scale(riparian_area_m2_dammed - riparian_area_m2_undammed)) +
        as.numeric(as.factor(surveyPeriod)) * 0.02
  ),
  size = 5, alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = bin_colors) +
  labs(
  x = expression(Delta*RIPA),
  y = expression(Delta*TO),
  title = expression(Delta * SWA * " Across " * Delta * TO * " and " * Delta * RIPA),
  subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years",
  color = expression(Delta*SWA)
) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```





```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
lt_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = dam_m_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Dam Density (" *DamCount[i]/streamLength(m)[i]* ")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Lone Tree Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


```{r,echo=TRUE}
ltRI_Flow <- lt_sample_pairs %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         RI_dammed,
         RI_undammed) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(RI_dammed, RI_undammed),
    names_to = "window_type",
    values_to = "RI"
  )

ggplot(ltRI_Flow,
       aes(x = zFlow_mean,
           y = RI,
           color = as.factor(window_type))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RI_dammed" = "brown",
                                "RI_undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    color = "Window Type",
    title = "Riparian Inundation Across Flow",
    subtitle = "LT All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```



----
----
----
----


----
----
----
----


BRAZIL
----
----

Compute **Riparian Inundation**

For survey window[i] at time[t], the proportion of riparian area that is covered in surface water

$SWA = Surface Water Area (m^2)$

$RIPA = Riparian Area (m^2)$

$$RI[i,t] = SWA[i,t] / RIPA[i] $$


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# COMPUTE RI HERE!!!
BRZ_RI <- BRZ %>% 
  mutate(RI = SWA/riparian_area_m2)
```

Visualize riparian inundation **(RI)** $SWA/RIPA$ between dammed and undammed across years.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
BRZ_RI %>%
  filter(!is.na(RI)) %>%   # optional but recommended
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year),
             y = RI,
             fill = window_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                               "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    fill = "Window type",
    title = "RI across dammed vs undammed windows",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek (2019, 2021, 2023)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
BRZ_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Brazil Ck; Summer 2019") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
BRZ_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2021) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Brazil Ck; Summer 2021") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
BRZ_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Brazil Ck; Summer 2023") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```


----

Add **relative flow data**. 

NOTE: currently this represents data from the USGS Guston Coulee gage that has been z-standardized over the current data acquisition range (June-Sept; 2019, 2021, 2023). 

Then, for each 2-week survey time period (1:8), the average relative flow $[mean(zFlow)]$ was computed, and added as a data column. IT IS ASSUMED that this flow data is representative of the RELATIVE flow pattern experienced throughout the study area during this time. Consider incorporating relative HHP simulated flow data again (?)

----

Visualize Surface Water Area across Relative Flow and Topographic Openness


```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
BRZ_RelFlow <- BRZ_RI %>% 
  left_join(mean_flow_per_survey_period,
            by = "surveyPeriod") %>% 
  mutate(dam_m = Nhat/window_length)


BRZ_RelFlow %>% 
  arrange(SWA) %>%
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO, 
           color = SWA,
           shape = window_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "mako",begin = 0,end   = 1,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("Surface Water Area" (m^2)),
    title = "Surface Water Area Across RelativeFlow and TO space",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


----

----  

----

----



Visualize covariate distributions b/t dammed and undammed windows:

```{r,include=FALSE}
ggplot(BRZ_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = TO, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


ggplot(BRZ_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = riparian_area_m2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(BRZ_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = SWA, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(BRZ_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = RI, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

ggplot(BRZ_RelFlow, 
       aes(x = catchment_km2, fill = window_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("undammed" = "steelblue", "dammed" = "brown")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


----

Visualize covariate correlations for Willow Creek :

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
#2019
brz19_numCols <- BRZ_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2019") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
brz19_corr_matrix <- cor(brz19_numCols)

# Create the plot
brz19_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(brz19_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Brazil Creek 2019") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

brz19_ggcorr_plot

#2021
brz21_numCols <- BRZ_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2021") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
brz21_corr_matrix <- cor(brz21_numCols)

# Create the plot
brz21_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(brz21_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Brazil Creek 2021") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

brz21_ggcorr_plot


#2023
brz23_numCols <- BRZ_RelFlow %>% 
  filter(year == "2023") %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         riparian_area_m2,
         window_length,
         SWA,
         RI,
         TO,
         Nhat,
         dam_m,
         catchment_km2) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(SWA))
# Correlation matrix
brz23_corr_matrix <- cor(brz23_numCols)

# Create the plot
brz23_ggcorr_plot <- ggcorrplot(brz23_corr_matrix, lab = TRUE, type = "upper")+
  ggtitle("Correlation matrix for Brazil Creek 2023") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

brz23_ggcorr_plot
```

----

----

----

Compute Differences (Dammed[i] - Undammed):
----

Compute difference in RI $(\Delta RI)$ between dammed and undammed pairs. The hypothesis is that dammed reaches will inundate more of their respective available space (m^2) than their paired undammed reaches. (+)$(\Delta RI)$ means the dammed site in a pair inundated more of its available riparian space than its undammed counterpart; (-) is vice versa.

The degree to which beaver dams influence $\Delta RI$ confounded by pairwise differences in reach characteristics (e.g., $TO$, $UDA$)

----

Proposed model:

$$\Delta RI[i] = \alpha + \beta1 * \Delta UDA[i] + \beta2 * \Delta TO + \beta3 *  Nhat[i_{\text{dammed}}]$$


Wherein, the difference in riparian inundation $(\Delta RI)$ for sample pair $[i]$ is a linear function of the pair's difference in stream network position $(\Delta UDA)$, the pair's difference in local channel confinement $(\Delta TO)$, and the pair's difference in number of dams $(\Delta Nhat)$ (NOTE: delta_Nhat is just Nhat of the dammed site in pair [i]).

Consider, instead of Nhat, using $dam/m = Nhat/windowLength (m)$ to get at dam density within the window, rather than raw dam count.


```{r,echo=FALSE}
brz_sample_pairs <- BRZ_RelFlow %>%
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         window_type, 
         RI, 
         TO, 
         riparian_area_m2, 
         zFlow_mean,
         catchment_km2,
         Nhat,
         dam_m) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = window_type,
    values_from = c(RI, TO, riparian_area_m2, catchment_km2, Nhat,dam_m),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(delta_RI = RI_dammed - RI_undammed)

brz_sample_differences <- brz_sample_pairs %>%
  mutate(
    delta_TO   = TO_dammed - TO_undammed,
    delta_RIPA = riparian_area_m2_dammed - riparian_area_m2_undammed,
    delta_UDA  = catchment_km2_dammed - catchment_km2_undammed,
    delta_Nhat = Nhat_dammed,
    delta_dam_m = dam_m_dammed
  ) %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         year,
         t,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         delta_RI,
         delta_TO,
         delta_RIPA,
         delta_UDA,
         delta_Nhat,
         delta_dam_m
         )

str(brz_sample_differences)
```


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across $\Delta Nhat$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

brz_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)


#####

brz_sample_differences %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = factor(round(delta_Nhat)),   # <-- THIS is the key
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("(" * Delta * " Dam Count)"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Difference in Dam Count",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```


----


----

Visualize $\Delta RI$ across **damDensity** $(dam/m)$

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2019') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2019"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

brz_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2021') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2021"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####

brz_sample_differences %>% 
  filter(year=='2023') %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = delta_dam_m,
      y = delta_RI,
      colour = zFlow_mean
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Dam Density (" * DamCount[i]/StreamLength[i] * ")"),
    y = expression("(" * Delta * " RI)"),
    title = "Difference in Riparian Inundation by Dam Density (Dam/Stream_m)",
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek 2023"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

#####
```


----

Dig into where $\Delta RI >0$; these illustrate contexts where dams may be positively influencing SWA.

Look at TO and RIPA and UDA for delta_RI > 0 dam clusters

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_posDamDiff <- brz_sample_pairs %>% 
  filter(delta_RI > 0)

brz_posDamDiff$year <- as.factor(brz_posDamDiff$year)

brz_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Topographic Openness (TO)",
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and TO"),
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)



brz_sample_differences %>%
  filter(delta_RI >0) %>% 
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = delta_TO, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = as.factor(year))) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Difference in Topographic Openness (  "*Delta*" TO)"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    shape = "Year",
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and " *Delta* "TO"),
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = riparian_area_m2_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Riparian Area ("*m^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and RIPA"),
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = catchment_km2_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Upstream Drainage Area ("*km^2*")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```

```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
brz_posDamDiff %>%
  arrange(delta_RI) %>% 
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = dam_m_dammed, 
           color = delta_RI,
           shape = year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno",begin = 0,end   = 0.875,direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = expression("Dam Density (" *DamCount[i]/streamLength(m)[i]* ")"),
    color = expression("("*Delta*" RI) (RI_dammed - RI_undammed)"),
    title = expression("Sites with Positive deltaRI Across Flow and UDA"),
    subtitle = "Brazil Creek All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


```{r,echo=TRUE}
brzRI_Flow <- brz_sample_pairs %>% 
  select(clusterID_seq,
         surveyPeriod,
         zFlow_mean,
         RI_dammed,
         RI_undammed) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(RI_dammed, RI_undammed),
    names_to = "window_type",
    values_to = "RI"
  )

ggplot(brzRI_Flow,
       aes(x = zFlow_mean,
           y = RI,
           color = as.factor(window_type))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RI_dammed" = "brown",
                                "RI_undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = "z-Standardized Flow",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    color = "Window Type",
    title = "Riparian Inundation Across Flow",
    subtitle = "BRZ All Years"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
```


----
----
----
----




----
----
----
----


LARB
----
----

Compute **Riparian Inundation**

For survey window[i] at time[t], the proportion of riparian area that is covered in surface water

$SWA = Surface Water Area (m^2)$

$RIPA = Riparian Area (m^2)$

$$RI[i,t] = SWA[i,t] / RIPA[i] $$


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# COMPUTE RI HERE!!!
LRB_RI <- LRB %>% 
  mutate(RI = SWA/riparian_area_m2)
```

Visualize riparian inundation **(RI)** $SWA/RIPA$ between dammed and undammed across years.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
LRB_RI %>%
  filter(!is.na(RI)) %>%   # optional but recommended
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year),
             y = RI,
             fill = window_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                               "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Riparian Inundation (RI)",
    fill = "Window type",
    title = "RI across dammed vs undammed windows",
    subtitle = "Larb Creek (2019, 2021, 2023)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
LRB_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Larb Ck; Summer 2019") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
LRB_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2021) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Larb Ck; Summer 2021") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
LRB_RI %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
     ggplot(aes(x = t,
                y = RI,
                color = window_type)) +
     geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
     geom_smooth(method = "loess",
                 se = TRUE,
                 linewidth = 1.2) +
     scale_color_manual(values = c("dammed" = "brown",
                                   "undammed" = "steelblue")) +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8,
                        labels = paste0("t", 1:8)) +
     labs(x = "Time Period (t)",
          y = "Riparian Inundation (SWA/RIPA)",
          color = "Window Type",
          title = "Riparian proportion inundated over time",
          subtitle = "Larb Ck; Summer 2023") +
     theme_bw(base_size = 15)

```


----

Add **relative flow data**. 

NOTE: currently this represents data from the USGS Guston Coulee gage that has been z-standardized over the current data acquisition range (June-Sept; 2019, 2021, 2023). 

Then, for each 2-week survey time period (1:8), the average relative flow $[mean(zFlow)]$ was computed, and added as a data column. IT IS ASSUMED that this flow data is representative of the RELATIVE flow pattern experienced throughout the study area during this time. Consider incorporating relative HHP simulated flow data again (?)

----

Visualize Surface Water Area across Relative Flow and Topographic Openness


```{r,echo=FALSE, fig.width=10}
LRB_RelFlow <- LRB_RI %>% 
  left_join(mean_flow_per_survey_period,
            by = "surveyPeriod") %>% 
  mutate(dam_m = Nhat/window_length)


LRB_RelFlow %>% 
  arrange(SWA) %>%
  ggplot(
       aes(x = zFlow_mean, 
           y = TO, 
           color = SWA,
           shape = window_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
```
