```{r, echo=FALSE}
  n_event = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$sampleEvent)),
  n_year = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$year_id)),
  n_cluster = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$clusterID_num)),
  sd_SWA = sd(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$SWA, na.rm = T)
)

parameters_m2 <- c(
  "alpha",
  "beta1",
  "b_stream",
  "b_year",
  "beta2",
  "beta3",
  "beta4",
  "sigma",
  "eta_stream",
  "eta_event",
  "eta_cluster",
  "sigma_stream",
  "sigma_b_stream",
  "sigma_b_year",
  "sigma_event",
  "sigma_cluster"
)

m2_HGLM_SWA <- jags(data = m2_data_list,
                   inits = inits,
                   parameters.to.save = parameters_m2,
                   model.file = "m2_HGLM_SWA.jags",
                   parallel = T,
                   n.chains = nchains,
                   n.thin = nthin,
                   n.iter = niter,
                   n.burnin = nburn)


Sys.time()

print(m2_HGLM_SWA)

```
difference in sys.time = elapsed time for model to run


**NOTE:**
----

In M2, TO was transformed to "channel incision" by taking the inverse of proportion openness:

$$ChannelIncision = 1 - (TO/90) $$

Where $(TO/90)$ is the proportion of max openness (90 degrees = "flat"/"fully open")




```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Extract posteriors for parameters of interest

#library(dplyr)
#library(tidyr)
#library(ggplot2)
#library(purrr)

# Extract posterior draws
M2post <- m2_HGLM_SWA$sims.list

# Select only scalar parameters (convert random effects matrices)
# Scalars
M2df_scalar <- tibble(
  parameter = c("alpha", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3", "beta4"),
  mean = c(mean(M2post$alpha), 
           mean(M2post$beta1), 
           mean(M2post$beta2), 
           mean(M2post$beta3),
           mean(M2post$beta4)),
  lower = c(quantile(M2post$alpha, 0.025), 
            quantile(M2post$beta1, 0.025),
            quantile(M2post$beta2, 0.025),
            quantile(M2post$beta3, 0.025),
            quantile(M2post$beta4, 0.025)),
  upper = c(quantile(M2post$alpha, 0.975), 
            quantile(M2post$beta1, 0.975),
            quantile(M2post$beta2, 0.975),
            quantile(M2post$beta3, 0.975),
            quantile(M2post$beta4, 0.975))
)

# Stream-level slopes
M2df_stream <- map_df(1:ncol(M2post$b_stream), function(j){
  tibble(
    parameter = paste0("b_stream[", j, "]"),
    mean = mean(M2post$b_stream[, j]),
    lower = quantile(M2post$b_stream[, j], 0.025),
    upper = quantile(M2post$b_stream[, j], 0.975)
  )
})

# Year-level slopes
M2df_year <- map_df(1:ncol(M2post$b_year), function(y){
  tibble(
    parameter = paste0("b_year[", y, "]"),
    mean = mean(M2post$b_year[, y]),
    lower = quantile(M2post$b_year[, y], 0.025),
    upper = quantile(M2post$b_year[, y], 0.975)
  )
})

M2summary_df <- bind_rows(M2df_scalar, M2df_stream, M2df_year)

# Give parameters meaningful names
pretty_names <- c(
  alpha           = "Intercept",
  beta1           = "Dam Presence",
  beta2           = "Topographic Openness",
  beta3           = "Riparian Area (m^2)",
  beta4           = "Contributing Drainage Area",
  "b_stream[1]"   = "Brazil Ck",
  "b_stream[2]"   = "Lower Beaver Ck",
  "b_stream[3]"   = "Larb Ck",
  "b_stream[4]"   = "Lone Tree Ck",
  "b_stream[5]"   = "Upper Beaver Ck",
  "b_stream[6]"   = "Willow Ck",
  "b_year[1]"     = "2019",
  "b_year[2]"     = "2021",
  "b_year[3]"     = "2023"
)


# Plot
ggplot(M2summary_df %>% filter(parameter == "beta1" |
                               parameter == "beta2" |
                               parameter == "beta3" |
                               parameter == "beta4"), 
       aes(x = parameter, 
           y = mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, 
                    ymax = upper), width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = pretty_names) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-100, 800)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = expression("Model 2: (" * SWA * " ~ " * Dam_Presence * ") Considering Geomorphic Context" ),
    subtitle = "Posterior Means and 95% Credible Intervals",
    x = "Parameter",
    y = "Posterior Mean"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12))

```

M2 Mean Dam Effect:

Same interpretation as M1

```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m2_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta1))
```

M2 Mean TO Effect

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in TO

```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m2_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta2))
```

M2 Mean RIPA Effect:

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in RIPA


```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m2_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta3))
```

M2 Mean CDA Effect

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in CDA


```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m2_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta4))
```



```{r,echo=FALSE}
# HEAT MAP IT

M2n_sims   <- length(M2post$beta1)
M2n_stream <- ncol(M2post$b_stream)
M2n_year   <- ncol(M2post$b_year)

#range(M2post$b_stream[,3] + M2post$b_year[,2] + M2post$beta1)


M2DamEffect <- array(NA, dim = c(M2n_sims, 
                                 M2n_stream, 
                                 M2n_year))

for(j in 1:M2n_stream){
  for(y in 1:M2n_year){
    M2DamEffect[, j, y] <- M2post$beta1 +
                           M2post$b_stream[, j] +
                           M2post$b_year[, y]
  }
}

```


```{r,echo=FALSE}
#Summarize posteriors for each 

M2summary_list <- list()

for(j in 1:M2n_stream){
  for(y in 1:M2n_year){
    
    eff <- as.vector(M2DamEffect[, j, y, drop = FALSE])
    
    M2summary_list[[paste(j, y, sep = "_")]] <- tibble(
      stream = j,
      year = y,
      mean = mean(eff),
      lower = quantile(eff, 0.025),
      upper = quantile(eff, 0.975),
      positive = quantile(eff, 0.025) > 0
    )
  }
}


M2effect_df <- bind_rows(M2summary_list) %>%
  left_join(stream_lookup, by = c("stream")) %>%
  left_join(year_lookup,  by = c("year" = "year_id")) %>%
  rename(year_id = year) %>% 
  left_join(uda_lookup, by = "watershed")

# order watersheds by factor levels of ascending max UDA
M2effect_df <- M2effect_df %>%
  mutate(watershed = factor(
    watershed,
    levels = c("WC", "LT", "BRZ", "LRB", "LBV", "UBV")
  ))


# Plot the heatmap

ggplot(M2effect_df, aes(x = as.factor(year.y), 
                        y = watershed, 
                        fill = mean)) +
  geom_tile(color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "grey90",
    mid = "grey100",
    high = "dodgerblue4",
    midpoint = 0,
    name = "Model 2 
Dam Effect"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Watershed 
Arranged By Drainage Area",
    title = "Magnitude of Dam Effect by Watershed and Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

^^^ Less dam effect overall, still lots of variation among watersheds and years

Also, streams are ordered here ^^ by contributing drainage area. Higher on the y axis = greater drainage area





Visualize proportional effect of beaver dams on riparian inundation
----

'average dam inundated ~2% of available riparian space'

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Posterior draws of dam effect (population-level)
dam_post <- M2post$beta1   # vector of length ~4500

# Median riparian area (real units)
RIPA_med <- median(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$riparian_area_m2)

# Posterior distribution of proportional inundation
prop_post <- dam_post / RIPA_med

# Summaries
quantile(prop_post, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))


prop_df1 <- tibble(prop = prop_post)

ggplot(prop_df1, aes(x = prop)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(prop_post), color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_post, 0.025), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_post, 0.975), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Proportion of Riparian Area Inundated by Dams",
    y = "Posterior Density",
    title = "Posterior Distribution of Proportional Riparian Inundation",
    subtitle = expression("Based on population-level dam effect ( " * beta * 1 * " ) and median riparian area")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)



```

Check proportional change from undammed to dammed
----

```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Compute predicted baseline for 'typical' reach for each stream/year

TO_z_med <- median(as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$TO)))

RIPA_z_med <- median(as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$riparian_area_m2)))

UDA_z_med <- median(as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod$catchment_km2)))

post <- M2post

SWA0_global <- post$alpha +
  post$beta2 * TO_z_med +
  post$beta3 * RIPA_z_med +
  post$beta4 * UDA_z_med

SWA0_global <- pmax(SWA0_global, 0)  # enforce SWA ≥ 0


# global effect

prop_global <- post$beta1 / SWA0_global
quantile(prop_global, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))


prop_df <- tibble(prop = prop_global)

ggplot(prop_df, aes(x = prop)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(prop_global), color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_global, 0.025), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_global, 0.975), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Proportional Change in SWA (Dammed vs Undammed)",
    y = "Posterior Density",
    title = "Global Proportional Effect of Beaver Dams",
    subtitle = "Population-level dam effect relative to global baseline SWA"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)


```





----

----

----

----

----

----

----


This next section intends to explore seasonal variation in beaver dam effect. 

Uses nested random slopes for beaver dam effect to allow for stream, year, and 
sampleEvent to cause deviation from the global mean beaver effect.


Implement HGLMMs:
----


M3: Dam Presence Only; seasonal random slope (sampleEvent)
----


```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(jagsUI)

Sys.time()

sink("m3_HGLM_SWA.jags")

cat("
model {

  for (i in 1:N) {
    SWA[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], tau)

  mu[i] <- alpha
             + (beta1
                + b_stream_event[stream[i],sampleEvent[i]]) * damPresence[i]
             + eta_stream[stream[i]]
             + eta_event[sampleEvent[i]]
             + eta_cluster[clusterID[i]]
  }

  # Fixed effects
  alpha ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta1 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)

  # Random Effects
  # Stream/Watershed
  for (j in 1:n_stream) {
    eta_stream[j] ~ dnorm(0, tau_stream)     # random intercept for stream
    b_stream[j]   ~ dnorm(0, tau_b_stream)   # random slope for stream
  }
  
  # Year-level dam effect
  for(yr in 1:n_year) {
    b_year[yr] ~ dnorm(0, tau_b_year)        # random slope for year
  }
  
  # Event-level dam effect nested within year
  for (k in 1:n_event) {
    b_event[k] ~ dnorm(b_year[event_year[k]],tau_b_event)       # random slope for year/survey 'sampling event'
    eta_event[k] ~ dnorm(0, tau_event)
  }
  
  # Stream/Event Interaction
  for(j in 1:n_stream){
    for(k in 1:n_event){
      b_stream_event[j,k] ~ dnorm(b_event[k] + b_stream[j], tau_b_stream_event)
    }
  }
  
  # Cluster ID
  for (c in 1:n_cluster) {
    eta_cluster[c] ~ dnorm(0, tau_cluster)
  }

  # Hyperpriors
  sigma ~ dnorm(0, 1 / pow(sd_SWA, 2)) T(0,)   # half-normal with sd ≈ sd_SWA
  tau <- pow(sigma, -2)

  sigma_stream ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_stream <- pow(sigma_stream, -2)

  sigma_b_stream ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_b_stream <- pow(sigma_b_stream, -2)
  
  sigma_b_year ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_b_year <- pow(sigma_b_year, -2)

  sigma_b_event ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_b_event <- pow(sigma_b_event, -2)
  
  sigma_event ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_event <- pow(sigma_event, -2)
  
  sigma_b_stream_event ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_b_stream_event <- pow(sigma_b_stream_event, -2)
  
  sigma_cluster ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_cluster <- pow(sigma_cluster, -2)
}
", fill = TRUE)

sink()


m3_data_list <- list(
  N = nrow(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4),
  SWA = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$SWA,
  damPresence = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$damPresence,
  stream = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$stream,
  clusterID = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$clusterID_num,
  n_stream = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$stream)),
  n_year = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$year_id)),
  n_cluster = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$clusterID_num)),
  sampleEvent = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$sampleEvent_new,
  n_event = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$sampleEvent_new)),
  event_year = event_year_3_4,
  sd_SWA = sd(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$SWA, na.rm = T)
)

m3inits <- function(){list(
    alpha = rnorm(1, 0, 1),
    beta1 = rnorm(1, 0, 1)
    )}

parameters_m3 <- c(
  "alpha",
  "beta1",
  "b_stream",
  "b_year",
  "b_event",
  "b_stream_event",
  "sigma",
  "eta_stream",
  "eta_event",
  "eta_cluster",
  "sigma_stream",
  "sigma_b_stream",
  "sigma_b_year",
  "sigma_b_event",
  "sigma_b_stream_event",
  "sigma_event",
  "sigma_cluster"
)

nchains <- 3
nthin <- 10
niter <- 20000
nburn <- 5000

m3_HGLM_SWA <- jags(data = m3_data_list,
                   inits = m3inits,
                   parameters.to.save = parameters_m3,
                   model.file = "m3_HGLM_SWA.jags",
                   parallel = T,
                   n.chains = nchains,
                   n.thin = nthin,
                   n.iter = niter,
                   n.burnin = nburn)


Sys.time()

print(m3_HGLM_SWA) # model with local and watershed dam density as fixed effects

```
difference in sys.time = elapsed time for model to run

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Extract posteriors for parameters of interest

#library(dplyr)
#library(tidyr)
#library(ggplot2)
#library(purrr)

# Extract posterior draws
M3post <- m3_HGLM_SWA$sims.list

# Select only scalar parameters (convert random effects matrices)
# Scalars
M3df_scalar <- tibble(
  parameter = c("alpha", "beta1"),
  mean = c(mean(M3post$alpha), 
           mean(M3post$beta1)),
  lower = c(quantile(M3post$alpha, 0.025), 
            quantile(M3post$beta1, 0.025)),
  upper = c(quantile(M3post$alpha, 0.975), 
            quantile(M3post$beta1, 0.975))
)

# Stream-level slopes
M3df_stream <- map_df(1:ncol(M3post$b_stream), function(j){
  tibble(
    parameter = paste0("b_stream[", j, "]"),
    mean = mean(M3post$b_stream[, j]),
    lower = quantile(M3post$b_stream[, j], 0.025),
    upper = quantile(M3post$b_stream[, j], 0.975)
  )
})

# Season-level slopes
M3df_event <- map_df(1:ncol(M3post$b_event), function(y){
  tibble(
    parameter = paste0("b_event[", y, "]"),
    mean = mean(M3post$b_event[, y]),
    lower = quantile(M3post$b_event[, y], 0.025),
    upper = quantile(M3post$b_event[, y], 0.975)
  )
})

M3summary_df <- bind_rows(M3df_scalar, M3df_stream, M3df_event)

# Give parameters meaningful names
pretty_names <- c(
  alpha           = "Intercept",
  beta1           = "Dam Presence",
  "b_stream[1]"   = "Brazil Ck",
  "b_stream[2]"   = "Lower Beaver Ck",
  "b_stream[3]"   = "Larb Ck",
  "b_stream[4]"   = "Lone Tree Ck",
  "b_stream[5]"   = "Upper Beaver Ck",
  "b_stream[6]"   = "Willow Ck",
  "b_event[1]"     = "19_t1",
  "b_event[2]"     = "19_t2",
  "b_event[3]"     = "19_t3",
  "b_event[4]"     = "19_t4",
  "b_event[5]"     = "19_t5",
  "b_event[6]"     = "19_t6",
  "b_event[7]"     = "19_t7",
  "b_event[8]"     = "19_t8",
  "b_event[9]"     = "21_t1",
  "b_event[10]"     = "21_t2",
  "b_event[11]"     = "21_t3",
  "b_event[12]"     = "21_t4",
  "b_event[13]"     = "21_t5",
  "b_event[14]"     = "21_t6",
  "b_event[15]"     = "21_t7",
  "b_event[16]"     = "21_t8",
  "b_event[17]"     = "23_t5",
  "b_event[18]"     = "23_t6",
  "b_event[19]"     = "23_t7",
  "b_event[20]"     = "23_t8"
)


# Plot
ggplot(M3summary_df, 
       aes(x = parameter, 
           y = mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, 
                    ymax = upper), width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = pretty_names) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = expression("Model 3: " * SWA ~ Dam_Presence ),
    subtitle = "Posterior Means and 95% Credible Intervals",
    x = "Parameter",
    y = "Posterior Mean"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12))

print(mean(m3_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta1))
```


^^^ Average dam effect across the study area is 283.9. This translates to:

"On average, dammed reaches pond about 284 m^2 more surface water than undammed 
reaches."


----


```{r,echo=FALSE}
# HEAT MAP IT

M3n_sims   <- length(M3post$beta1)
M3n_stream <- dim(M3post$b_stream_event)[2]
M3n_event   <- dim(M3post$b_stream_event)[3]

#range(M3post$b_stream[,3] + M3post$b_year[,2] + M3post$beta1)


M3DamEffect <- array(NA, dim = c(M3n_sims, 
                                 M3n_stream, 
                                 M3n_event))

for(j in 1:M3n_stream){
  for(e in 1:M3n_event){
    M3DamEffect[, j, e] <- M3post$beta1 +
                           M3post$b_stream_event[,j,e]
  }
}

```


```{r,echo=FALSE}
#Summarize posteriors for each 

M3summary_list <- list()

for(j in 1:M3n_stream){
  for(e in 1:M3n_event){
    
    eff <- as.vector(M3DamEffect[, j, e, drop = FALSE])
    
    M3summary_list[[paste(j, e, sep = "_")]] <- tibble(
      stream = j,
      event = e,
      mean = mean(eff),
      lower = quantile(eff, 0.025),
      upper = quantile(eff, 0.975),
      positive = quantile(eff, 0.025) > 0
    )
  }
}

# Build explicit lookups from the data actually used in the model
stream_lookup <- aoi_GLM_Data_mod %>%
  distinct(stream, watershed) %>%
  arrange(stream)

# Event lookup created above

M3effect_df <- bind_rows(M3summary_list) %>%
  left_join(stream_lookup, by = c("stream")) %>%
  left_join(event_lookup,  by = c("event" = "new"))

# order watersheds by factor levels of ascending max CDA
M3effect_df <- M3effect_df %>%
  mutate(watershed = factor(watershed, levels = uda_lookup$watershed))



# Plot the heatmap

ggplot(M3effect_df, aes(x = as.factor(event), 
                        y = watershed, 
                        fill = mean)) +
  geom_tile(color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "white",
    mid = "cornflowerblue",
    high = "navy",
    midpoint = 186,
    name = "Dam Effect"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sampling Event",
    y = "Watershed",
    title = "M3: Magnitude of Beaver Dam Effect on Surface Water Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```


^^^^ With SUBSTANTIAL variation among watersheds and years



Compare b1 trajectory across event, by stream:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
M3line_df <- M3effect_df %>%
  arrange(watershed, event) %>%
  mutate(event = as.numeric(event)) %>%
  group_by(watershed) %>%
  do({
    df <- .

    gap_rows <- tibble(
      stream = df$stream[1],
      event = c(4.5, 8.5),   # ← add as many gaps as you want
      mean = NA,
      lower = NA,
      upper = NA,
      positive = NA,
      watershed = df$watershed[1]
    )

    bind_rows(df, gap_rows)
  }) %>%
  arrange(watershed, event)



ggplot(M3line_df, aes(x = event, y = mean, color = watershed, group = watershed)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = unique(M3effect_df$event),
    labels = unique(event_lookup$label)   # if you want "19_t1", "19_t2", etc.
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Trajectory of Beaver Dam Effect on SWA, by Watershed",
    subtitle = "Gaps represent breaks by Year; t1:t4 excluded for 2023",
    x = "Sampling Events (t5 : t8) \n (August : September) \n (2019, 2021, 2023)",
    y = "Dam Effect (SWA m²)",
    color = "Watershed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```



----

----

----

M4: Dam Presence + Context with updated random effects structure
----


```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(jagsUI)

Sys.time()

sink("m4_HGLM_SWA.jags")

cat("
    model {

  for (i in 1:N) {
    SWA[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], tau)

  mu[i] <- alpha
             + (beta1
                + b_stream_event[stream[i],sampleEvent[i]]) * damPresence[i]
             + beta2 * TO[i] 
             + beta3 * RIPA[i]
             + beta4 * UDA[i]
             + eta_stream[stream[i]]
             + eta_event[sampleEvent[i]]
             + eta_cluster[clusterID[i]]
  }

  # Fixed effects
  alpha ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta1 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta2 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta3 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta4 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)

  # Random Effects
  # Stream/Watershed
  for (j in 1:n_stream) {
    eta_stream[j] ~ dnorm(0, tau_stream)     # random intercept for stream
    b_stream[j]   ~ dnorm(0, tau_b_stream)   # random slope for stream
  }
  
  # Year-level dam effect
  for(yr in 1:n_year) {
    b_year[yr] ~ dnorm(0, tau_b_year)        # random slope for year
  }
  
  # Event-level dam effect nested within year
  for (k in 1:n_event) {
    b_event[k] ~ dnorm(b_year[event_year[k]],tau_b_event)       # random slope for year/survey 'sampling event'
    eta_event[k] ~ dnorm(0, tau_event)
  }
  
  # Stream/Event Interaction
  for(j in 1:n_stream){
    for(k in 1:n_event){
      b_stream_event[j,k] ~ dnorm(b_event[k] + b_stream[j], tau_b_stream_event)
    }
  }
  
  # Cluster ID
  for (c in 1:n_cluster) {
    eta_cluster[c] ~ dnorm(0, tau_cluster)
  }

  # Hyperpriors
  sigma ~ dnorm(0, 1 / pow(sd_SWA, 2)) T(0,)   # half-normal with sd ≈ sd_SWA
  tau <- pow(sigma, -2)

  sigma_stream ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_stream <- pow(sigma_stream, -2)

  sigma_b_stream ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_b_stream <- pow(sigma_b_stream, -2)
  
  sigma_b_year ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_b_year <- pow(sigma_b_year, -2)

  sigma_b_event ~ dunif(0, 10)
  tau_b_event <- pow(sigma_b_event, -2)
  
  sigma_event ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_event <- pow(sigma_event, -2)
  
  sigma_b_stream_event ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_b_stream_event <- pow(sigma_b_stream_event, -2)
  
  sigma_cluster ~ dunif(0,10)
  tau_cluster <- pow(sigma_cluster, -2)
}
", fill = TRUE)

sink()


m4_data_list <- list(
  N = nrow(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4),
  SWA = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$SWA,
  damPresence = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$damPresence,
  TO = as.numeric(scale(1-(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$TO/90))),
  RIPA = as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$riparian_area_m2)),
  UDA = as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$catchment_km2)),
  stream = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$stream,
  clusterID = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$clusterID_num,
  n_stream = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$stream)),
  n_year = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$year_id)),
  n_cluster = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$clusterID_num)),
  sampleEvent = aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$sampleEvent_new,
  n_event = length(unique(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$sampleEvent_new)),
  event_year = event_year_3_4,
  sd_SWA = sd(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$SWA, na.rm = TRUE)
)

m4inits <- function(){list(
    alpha = rnorm(1, 0, 1),
    beta1 = rnorm(1, 0, 1)
    )}

parameters_m4 <- c(
  "alpha",
  "beta1",
  "beta2",
  "beta3",
  "beta4",
  "b_stream",
  "b_year",
  "b_event",
  "b_stream_event",
  "sigma",
  "eta_stream",
  "eta_event",
  "eta_cluster",
  "sigma_stream",
  "sigma_b_stream",
  "sigma_b_year",
  "sigma_b_event",
  "sigma_b_stream_event",
  "sigma_event",
  "sigma_cluster"
)

nchains <- 3
nthin <- 10
niter <- 20000
nburn <- 5000

m4_HGLM_SWA <- jags(data = m4_data_list,
                   inits = m4inits,
                   parameters.to.save = parameters_m4,
                   model.file = "m4_HGLM_SWA.jags",
                   parallel = T,
                   n.chains = nchains,
                   n.thin = nthin,
                   n.iter = niter,
                   n.burnin = nburn)


Sys.time()

print(m4_HGLM_SWA) # model with local and watershed dam density as fixed effects
```
difference in sys.time = elapsed time for model to run


**NOTE:**
----

In M4, TO was transformed to "channel incision" by taking the inverse of proportion openness:

$$ChannelIncision = 1 - (TO/90) $$

Where $(TO/90)$ is the proportion of max openness (90 degrees = "flat"/"fully open")




```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Extract posteriors for parameters of interest

#library(dplyr)
#library(tidyr)
#library(ggplot2)
#library(purrr)

# Extract posterior draws
m4post <- m4_HGLM_SWA$sims.list

# Select only scalar parameters (convert random effects matrices)
# Scalars
m4df_scalar <- tibble(
  parameter = c("alpha", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3", "beta4"),
  mean = c(mean(m4post$alpha), 
           mean(m4post$beta1), 
           mean(m4post$beta2), 
           mean(m4post$beta3),
           mean(m4post$beta4)),
  lower = c(quantile(m4post$alpha, 0.025), 
            quantile(m4post$beta1, 0.025),
            quantile(m4post$beta2, 0.025),
            quantile(m4post$beta3, 0.025),
            quantile(m4post$beta4, 0.025)),
  upper = c(quantile(m4post$alpha, 0.975), 
            quantile(m4post$beta1, 0.975),
            quantile(m4post$beta2, 0.975),
            quantile(m4post$beta3, 0.975),
            quantile(m4post$beta4, 0.975))
)


m4summary_df <- m4df_scalar

# Give parameters meaningful names
pretty_names <- c(
  alpha           = "Intercept",
  beta1           = "Dam Presence",
  beta2           = "Channel Incision",
  beta3           = "Riparian Area (m^2)",
  beta4           = "Contributing Drainage Area"
)


# Plot
ggplot(
  m4summary_df %>% 
    filter(parameter %in% c("beta1", "beta2", "beta3", "beta4")),
  aes(x = parameter, y = mean)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = pretty_names) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = expression("Model 4: (" * SWA * " ~ " * Dam_Presence * ") + Geomorphic Context"),
    subtitle = "Posterior Means and 95% Credible Intervals",
    x = "Parameter",
    y = "Posterior Mean"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12))
```

m4 Mean Dam Effect:

Same interpretation as M1

```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m4_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta1))
```

m4 Mean TO Effect

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in TO

```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m4_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta2))
```

m4 Mean RIPA Effect:

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in RIPA


```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m4_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta3))
```

m4 Mean CDA Effect

Interpretation: SWA(m^2) change per 1 standard deviation increase in CDA


```{r,echo=FALSE}
print(mean(m4_HGLM_SWA$sims.list$beta4))
```



```{r,echo=FALSE}
# HEAT MAP IT

m4n_sims   <- length(m4post$beta1)
m4n_stream <- ncol(m4post$b_stream)
m4n_event   <- ncol(m4post$b_event)

#range(m4post$b_stream[,3] + m4post$b_year[,2] + m4post$beta1)


m4DamEffect <- array(NA, dim = c(m4n_sims, 
                                 m4n_stream, 
                                 m4n_event))

for(j in 1:m4n_stream){
  for(k in 1:m4n_event){
    m4DamEffect[, j, k] <-
      m4post$beta1 +
      m4post$b_stream[, j] +
      m4post$b_event[, k]
  }
}

```


```{r,echo=FALSE}
#Summarize posteriors for each 

m4summary_list <- list()

for(j in 1:m4n_stream){
  for(k in 1:m4n_event){
    
    eff <- as.vector(m4DamEffect[, j, k, drop = FALSE])
    
    m4summary_list[[paste(j, k, sep = "_")]] <- tibble(
      stream = j,
      event = k,
      mean = mean(eff),
      lower = quantile(eff, 0.025),
      upper = quantile(eff, 0.975),
      positive = quantile(eff, 0.025) > 0
    )
  }
}


m4effect_df <- bind_rows(m4summary_list) %>%
  left_join(stream_lookup, by = c("stream")) %>% 
  left_join(event_lookup, by = c("event" = "new"))


# Plot the heatmap

ggplot(m4effect_df, aes(x = as.factor(old), 
                        y = watershed, 
                        fill = mean)) +
  geom_tile(color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "white",
    mid = "cornflowerblue",
    high = "black",
    midpoint = 193,
    name = "Model 2 
Dam Effect"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample Event",
    y = "Watershed",
    title = "Magnitude of Dam Effect by Watershed and SampleEvent",
    subtitle = "Aug:Sep; 5:8 = 2019; 13:16 = 2021; 21:24 = 2023"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

^^^ Less dam effect overall, still lots of variation among watersheds and years

Also, streams are ordered here ^^ by contributing drainage area. Higher on the y axis = greater drainage area





Visualize proportional change from undammed to dammed
----

```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Compute predicted baseline for 'typical' reach for each stream/year

post <- m4post

# --- Recreate standardized covariates exactly as in m4_data_list ---

# Channel incision = 1 - (TO/90)
TO_ci <- 1 - (aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$TO / 90)
TO_z  <- as.numeric(scale(TO_ci))

RIPA_z <- as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$riparian_area_m2))
UDA_z  <- as.numeric(scale(aoi_GLM_Data_mod_3_4$catchment_km2))

# Medians of standardized covariates
TO_z_med   <- median(TO_z, na.rm = TRUE)
RIPA_z_med <- median(RIPA_z, na.rm = TRUE)
UDA_z_med  <- median(UDA_z, na.rm = TRUE)

# Global basline SWA
SWA0_global <- post$alpha +
  post$beta2 * TO_z_med +
  post$beta3 * RIPA_z_med +
  post$beta4 * UDA_z_med

SWA0_global <- pmax(SWA0_global, 0)  # enforce SWA ≥ 0


# global effect

prop_global <- post$beta1 / SWA0_global
quantile(prop_global, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))


prop_df <- tibble(prop = prop_global)




hist(SWA0_global,
     main = "Posterior Distribution of Global Baseline SWA",
     xlab = "Baseline SWA (m²)")



ggplot(prop_df, aes(x = prop)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(prop_global), color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_global, 0.025), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = quantile(prop_global, 0.975), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Proportional Change in SWA (Dammed vs Undammed)",
    y = "Posterior Density",
    title = "Global Proportional Effect of Beaver Dams (Model 4)",
    subtitle = "Population-level dam effect relative to global baseline SWA"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
